timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Anne's suspicion fades as Stewart humorously asks for lunch, disarming her with his unexpected request, making her laugh and soften toward him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Anne's suspicion gives way to amusement after Stew returns letters and makes a light-hearted request.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne thanks Stew for returning her brother's letters, and his humorous lunch request causes her to laugh and lose her hostile attitude.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne is amused and her initial suspicion has dissipated.
evidence_quotes: Anne is regarding him with searching scrutiny. She smiles. Her icy, belligerent attitude has vanished.
I won't even pretend it isn't a very great favor. I wish there was something I could do for you.

relation_updates: Anne's suspicion fades
Anne develops friendly interest in Stew

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stew compliments the caviar, invites Mrs. Schuyler to visit, and exits with a bow, leaving her silently furious while Anne watches.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Anne mediates by urging Stew to leave to appease her mother.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne, distressed and flustered, asks Stew to go and says she will explain to her mother.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To calm her mother and avoid further confrontation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne is left with her mother, who is silently furious after Stew's exit.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Anne must now explain Stew's presence to her angry mother.
evidence_quotes: Anne, distressed, exclaims, 'Oh, Mother!'
Anne, flustered, pleads, 'Please go. I'll explain to Mother.'

relation_updates: Anne prioritizes mother's approval over Stew's presence

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stew and Anne embrace on a divan, where he confesses his insecurities and feelings, she reassures him, and they share a passionate kiss, deepening their romantic connection under the intimate garden setting.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Anne deepens her romantic bond with Stew through intimate interaction on the roof garden.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne and Stew share a passionate kiss after Stew confesses his feelings and Anne reassures him, escalating their romantic connection.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne is in a passionate embrace with Stew, with their romantic bond intensified.
evidence_quotes: Stew and Anne enter from the direction of the house, hand in hand
their figures seen in silhouette as they fall into a passionate embrace
Anne smiles, happy and serene, and gently pinches his nose.
He abandons his attempt at practicality, sweeps her into his arms, and kisses her passionately.

relation_updates: Anne commits romantically to Stew

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 6、INT. CITY ROOM OF MORNING POST - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gallagher reacts with shock and leaves upon reading about Stew's marriage; Stew, overwhelmed by colleagues, searches for her as his focus shifts from celebration to concern.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Newlywed wife
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne's marriage to Stew is reported in the Tribune, making it public knowledge.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne's marital status as Stew's wife is publicly announced.
evidence_quotes: ANN SCHUYLER ELOPES WITH REPORTER
She gets married to one of our own reporters and the Tribune beat us to it!

relation_updates: Anne is married to Stew Smith

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Anne and Stew debate living together; he refuses to live in her house despite her affection, insisting it would undermine his independence and reputation, while she seeks closeness.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Defiant wife confronting her mother over her marriage
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne publicly embraces Stew and declares their marriage to her disapproving mother.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne is with Stew after her mother exits the room in anger.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Mother's ongoing disapproval of the marriage.
evidence_quotes: making a show of embracing him.
ANNE Now Mother, your attitude is perfectly ridiculous. It's done now. Stewart and I are married.

relation_updates: Anne defies her mother's disapproval
Anne publicly supports Stew
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Assertive and committed to her husband

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Anne and Stew debate living together; he refuses to live in her house despite her affection, insisting it would undermine his independence and reputation, while she seeks closeness.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Anne negotiating to live in her family house with Stew
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne proposes that she and Stew live in her family's large house, offering the left wing, but Stew refuses, stating he would not live in her house on her money due to pride and social concerns.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Anne aims to live with Stew in her family house.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Stew explicitly rejects Anne's proposal to live in her house.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Where the couple will live remains undecided.
evidence_quotes: ANNE (gently insistent) Don't you think it's silly of us to think of living there when we have this whole big house
ANNE Sure. We can have the whole left wing and be all by ourselves all the time.
ANNE But we don't need it, I'm sure.

relation_updates: Anne and Stew clash over domestic independence
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Anne is affectionate and gently insistent in her proposal.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stew criticizes Anne for being rude to Gallagher, calls her behavior insincere, and leaves to apologize; Anne angrily stops him, intensifying the tension.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Jealous wife
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne reacts with jealousy upon seeing Stew with Gallagher, displaying hostility through her comments and demeanor.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne is hostile and suspicious toward Gallagher, with tension evident in the interaction.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Anne's jealousy has surfaced, affecting her trust in Stew.
evidence_quotes: Anne is walking in direction of Stew and Gallagher. She stops suddenly when she sees them. Her body stiffens.
She is looking off with slightly narrowed eyes.
ANNE (bitter-sweet) You know, Stewart, you failed to mention that Miss Gallagher was a very beautiful young girl.

relation_updates: Anne's jealousy toward Stew surfaces
Anne antagonizes Gallagher

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mrs. Schuyler panics over a sensational headline about Anne's husband; Grayson arrives with the paper, mocking the story as damaging publicity, while confusion ensues over summoning staff.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife facing public scandal
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne reads the front-page newspaper article reporting that her husband attacked a reporter and claimed to 'wear the pants' in their marriage.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne is present as her mother reacts with horror and Grayson mocks the public scandal.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to handle the fallout from the scandal and Stew's behavior.
evidence_quotes: Anne turns paper and reads the article.

relation_updates: Anne's private marital issues become public
status_updates: Anne's marriage is subject to public scandal

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stew admits to striking a reporter for calling him 'Cinderella Man,' faces criticism from Mrs. Schuyler and Grayson, but gains Anne's support; she defends him, leading to Mrs. Schuyler's angry exit.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife defending husband against family criticism
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne defends Stew's actions to her family, siding with him despite their disapproval.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne has sided with Stew, leading to her mother's angry exit and Stew comforting her.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Deepening rift with her mother and family.
evidence_quotes: ANNE Is this true, Stewart? Did you really say it?
I'm glad you hit that reporter, Stewart. He deserved it.
Stew puts his arms around the crying Anne.

relation_updates: Anne prioritizes Stew over family loyalty
Anne's bond with her mother weakens

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stew rejects Mrs. Schuyler's hospitality, declares his departure, and leaves with Gallagher, prompting Anne's exasperated reaction.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife in marital breakdown
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stew demands Anne take his name as 'Mrs. Stew Smith' and leaves the house with Gallagher, ending their cohabitation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne is left alone after Stew's departure, with their marriage arrangement in dispute.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Anne will accept Stew's name change condition and if the marriage can continue.
evidence_quotes: That's ridiculous.
You're acting like a child.
ANNE (exasperated) Oh, Stewart!

relation_updates: Anne's marriage reaches a breaking point
Stew departs, rejecting Anne's world
status_updates: Stew moves out of the house

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stew reimagines his play's ending, having the protagonist choose the O'Brien girl over his wife; in a charged moment, he enacts the romantic scene with Gallagher, leading to an emotional, prolonged kiss.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife initiating divorce
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Anne instructs Grayson to initiate divorce discussions with Stew, including financial settlement.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To divorce Stew and arrange financial terms.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anne is pursuing divorce from Stew, with Stew refusing financial settlement.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Stew's refusal to accept financial terms from Anne.
evidence_quotes: Anne asked me to come and see you about the divorce.
She wants me to arrange the financial settlement.

relation_updates: Anne takes steps to legally end the marriage
status_updates: Anne is in the process of divorcing Stew

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Anne begins as a suspicious lady of the house but softens toward Stew after he returns her brother's love letters. She marries him despite family disapproval, but their marriage faces conflicts over social status, jealousy, and public scandals. Ultimately, after Stew leaves with another woman, Anne initiates divorce proceedings.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Anne's suspicion fades as Stewart humorously asks for lunch, disarming her with his unexpected request, making her laugh and soften toward him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Stew's friend and colleague who has unspoken romantic feelings for him
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gallagher hears Stew express romantic interest in a Junior Leaguer, reacts with jealousy, and engages in a sarcastic exchange about the woman.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gallagher is jealous and the friendship is strained as Stew grows resentful and leaves.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Gallagher's unrequited feelings for Stew and how to cope with his new romantic interest
evidence_quotes: Gallagher looks up in astonishment, questioning his sanity.
Gallagher watches, quizzical, suspicious, and a little jealous.
She teases him, saying he’s going to be poison to that Junior Leaguer from now on.
Stew replies he hopes not, since he has to call on her that morning.

relation_updates: Friendship with Stew strained due to her jealousy and his obliviousness
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sarcastic and jealous friend who hides her romantic feelings

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 6、INT. CITY ROOM OF MORNING POST - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gallagher reacts with shock and leaves upon reading about Stew's marriage; Stew, overwhelmed by colleagues, searches for her as his focus shifts from celebration to concern.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gallagher and Stew reconcile after Anne's cold behavior; Stew admits he never told Anne that Gallagher is a woman, which he finds odd.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stew reacts to learning Gallagher is a woman by mock-chivalrously kissing her hand, threatening her if she gets fresh, then leaves to check on the vases, leaving Gallagher stunned.

relation_updates: Stew confronts Mrs. Schuyler and rejects her hospitality.
Stew gives Anne an ultimatum about their marital identity.
status_updates: Stew has left the boarding house and is now with Gallagher.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Stew, feeling treated like an outsider, leaves after issuing an ultimatum about their surname.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Stew reimagines his play's ending, having the protagonist choose the O'Brien girl over his wife; in a charged moment, he enacts the romantic scene with Gallagher, leading to an emotional, prolonged kiss.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: writer reimagining his play's ending
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Stew decides to change the play's ending to have the protagonist choose the O'Brien girl and enacts the romantic scene by kissing Gallagher.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To finish the play with the new ending where the protagonist chooses the O'Brien girl
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Stew is emotionally involved with Gallagher after the prolonged kiss and is actively working on the revised play ending.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The emotional impact of the kiss on Stew and Gallagher's relationship and the play's completion
evidence_quotes: Suddenly a thought strikes him, and his face breaks into a broad grin.
He's got to go to the other girl.
To demonstrate the point, Stew takes her in his arms and kisses her.
They hold the kiss longer than is justified.

relation_updates: Stew and Gallagher share a passionate kiss, indicating a deeper emotional connection
status_updates: Stew has a new idea for the play's third act
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Stew is a passionate writer who acts on creative impulses, blending personal emotion with his work.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Stew, a reporter, pursues and marries Anne Schuyler, but faces conflict with her family and struggles with his identity as he is perceived as a kept man. His friendship with Gallagher and his career are strained by the marriage. After a series of conflicts, he leaves Anne, writes a play about his life, and shares a kiss with Gallagher, signaling a new beginning.
